title: Anchor-Based vs. Anchor-Free
concept: Anchor-free models simplify architectures and handle irregular object shapes by replacing preset box templates with direct boundary regression.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the purpose of the 'centerness' score in FCOS?

options.text: To suppress poor bounding box predictions made by pixels near object borders

exercises.explain: Correct! Centerness helps filter out low-quality boundary predictions from edge pixels.

cards.title: Choosing Your Weapon
cards.body: Now that we know both anchor-based and anchor-free models, which design should you choose for your next project? Let's stack them up side-by-side to compare their real-world trade-offs.

cards.title: Anchor-Based vs. Anchor-Free
cards.body: Anchor-based models (like SSD, Faster R-CNN) use predefined stencils, making them stable for uniform objects but complex to tune. Anchor-free models (like CenterNet, FCOS, YOLOv8) regress boundaries directly, offering simpler architectures, fewer hyperparameters, and better handling of irregular shapes.

cards.title: Off-the-Rack vs. Tailor-Made
cards.body: Anchor-based is like buying off-the-rack clothing sizes: fast, but might not fit unusual body shapes. Anchor-free is like a tailor measuring your body directly: it fits any irregular shape perfectly because it adapts on the spot.

cards.title: Medical Cell Detection
cards.body: A medical AI needs to detect white blood cells under a microscope. Since cells constantly squash and change shape, an anchor-free model (like CenterNet) is selected because anchor templates fail to capture these irregular stretching boundaries.

cards.title: Speed Trade-off Myth
cards.body: You might think anchor-free models are slower because they calculate pixel offsets. Fact: By removing the massive grid of anchor stencils, anchor-free models are often faster, use less memory, and are easier to compile for edge chips.

cards.title: Architectural Trade-offs
cards.body: Comparing deployment characteristics of the two styles:

left.text:
Preset bounding box stencils
High hyperparameter tuning need
Slightly more stable for rigid classes

right.text:
Direct offset regression
No anchor tuning required
Highly flexible for irregular shapes

exercises.prompt: Which type of detector is generally easier to configure when training on a new dataset with highly irregular object shapes?

options.text: Anchor-free detectors, because they do not require defining preset template box shapes
Anchor-based detectors, because they have more parameters
Linear classifiers, because they do not use coordinates

exercises.explain: Correct! Anchor-free detectors regress shapes directly, making them highly flexible for irregular objects.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Anchor-free models completely eliminate the need to tune anchor scale and aspect ratio hyperparameters.
exercises.explain: True. By predicting offsets directly from pixels, anchor-free designs remove anchor hyperparameters entirely.

exercises.sentence: While anchor-based models use predefined stencils, anchor-free models use direct ___ to find boundaries.
exercises.explain: Anchor-free detectors use direct regression from pixels to borders.

exercises.prompt: Why are anchor-free models often preferred for edge device deployment?

options.text: They have simpler network heads and require less post-processing box calculations
They only run on CPUs, which are cheaper
They compress the image size automatically during inference

exercises.explain: Correct! Simpler prediction heads make anchor-free models lightweight and easy to run on edge accelerators.

exercises.prompt: You are building a system to detect cracks in concrete bridges. The cracks are long, winding, and have completely unpredictable shapes. Which architecture should you choose?

options.text: An anchor-free model like FCOS, which regresses boundary offsets directly from pixels and adapts easily to irregular, winding crack shapes.
An anchor-based SSD model with square default boxes.
A classification network that tells you if the bridge is gray.
A two-stage detector with rigid rectangular anchors.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Anchor-free models excel at capturing irregular, winding features that cannot fit into standard box templates. Phase 8: Modern Object Detection is complete. Next up: Image Segmentation.
